expectations.text: NCR intake fields + reasoned severity call (Major)
expectations.evidence: Severity labeled 'Moderate' — not a value in the defined Critical/Major/Minor band system; reasoning is sound but the label itself is off-schema.

expectations.text: Walks §4.1 review checklist under correct SLA
expectations.evidence: Conversion decision reasoned in prose, not a numbered checklist walk; no SLA date given.

expectations.text: CAPA cover block + reasoned RCA tool
expectations.evidence: No structured CAPA cover block; RCA tool (5 Whys) is reasoned but picks the opposite tool from the skill's own systemic-pattern heuristic without acknowledging the tradeoff.
Actions listed in prose without owner/timeline/evidence columns; no SOP-revision auto-stage mechanic mentioned.
Does name 'Operator gate' and 'QA Lead sign-off on root cause findings' correctly, if informally.
Explicitly frames itself as evaluating the handler's handoff.

eval_feedback.overall: The invented 'Moderate' severity value is the most concrete miss — it doesn't exist in the SOP's severity vocabulary, which is exactly the kind of schema drift the skill exists to prevent.
